ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Apr-24-2025 Off Calendar

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Feb-18-2025 Continued To May-13-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

DEFAULT HEARING (PROVE UP)
Off Calendar Jan-31-2025 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter comes on calendar this day, being set for hearing on Plaintiff's request for entry of default judgment.

Dept. 505; Judge Michelle Tong; Clerk N Webb; Proceedings recorded by "For the Record" commencing at 9:15 am and concluding at 9:27 am.

Plaintiff Toby Nady is present via zoom video conference, represented by James Sturdevant, Esq.
Defendant Crystal Vasquez not appearing.

Plaintiff presents summary of facts, claims and an offer of proof.

TOBY NADY is examined by the Court.

Plaintiff makes oral request to dismiss doe defendants.
The Court Grants Plaintiff's oral request to dismiss doe defendants.

COURT FINDS:
1. The 3rd amended complaint filed August 19, 2022 is the operative pleading.
2. The 3rd amended complaint was properly served on March 29, 2023.
3. Default against Defendant entered on September 25, 2023.

The matter is submitted.
By the statements made by Plaintiff and its counsel, documents filed, and evidence presented, the Court finds that Plaintiff has provided sufficient evidence proving each element of the complaint.

Good cause appearing, the Court RULES in favor of Plaintiff Toby Nady, and against Defendant Crystal Vasquez, as follows:

1. Damages: $535,000.00
2. Costs: $555.00

TOTAL JUDGMENT: $535,555.00

Counsel for Plaintiff to submit proposed judgment for review and signature. (D505)

Off Calendar Nov-01-2022 Continued To Jan-04-23 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: DEFENDANT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'s Motion To Strike Plaintiffs Third Amended Complaint.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s motion to strike from the third amended complaint (TAC) is denied.

First, plaintiff states that it can seek CCP 1021.5 attorney fees (Opp. 6:8-19); this suffices at the pleading stage.

Second, plaintiff adequately pleads specific facts supporting punitive damages. (Id. at 2:22-4:7, 4:23-5:4, 6:7-10, 8:3:11; see also Not. 3:2-3.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)

DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-10-2022 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

HEARING RE: RESPONDENT WELLS FARGO BANK, N.A.'S DEMURRER TO AMENDED COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Wells Fargo Bank, N.A.'s demurrer to the third amended complaint (TAC) is overruled.

This court's August 4, 2022 order granted plaintiff leave to plead facts showing that Wells Fargo owed it duties of inquiry and disclosure "when checks, not insignificant in amount, are drawn payable to the order of a bank and are presented to the payee bank by a party seeking to negotiate the checks for his own benefit. (Kurtz-Ahlers, LLC v. Bank of Am., N.A. (2020) 48 Cal.App.5th 952, 957.)" (8/4/22 Order p.4.) The TAC so pleads, and a string of cases so holds. (See, e.g., id. at 8:7-11; Opp. 5:13-6:7.) Further, this is a concealment case in which fraud may be pled less specifically. (See Cansino v. Bank of America (2014) 224 Cal.App.4th 1462, 1469.) This court's August 2022 order also granted plaintiff leave to amend its UCL claim with a proper fraud predicate, and it has done so. (Id. at p.4.)

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr.; Clerk: Sean Kane; Not Reported. (302/RBU)
